Sarah Chen, PhD
Senior Data Scientist | NLP & LLM Systems

Berlin, Germany · +49 151 2847 6392
sarah.chen.ai@protonmail.com
https://www.linkedin.com/in/sarahchen-ds/ · https://github.com/schen-ml

SUMMARY
8+ years building production ML systems. Specialized in information retrieval, embedding models, and RAG pipelines. Previously scaled a semantic search product from prototype to 40M queries/month.

EXPERIENCE

Senior Data Scientist, Search & Relevance — Klarna · Berlin
Oct 2022 - Present
· Led the design of a hybrid BM25 + dense retrieval system (+14% nDCG vs previous baseline)
· Fine-tuned multilingual sentence encoders on 2M labeled query-product pairs
· Mentored 3 junior DS; owns the weekly relevance review

ML Engineer — Delivery Hero · Berlin
Jan 2020 - Sep 2022
· Built the restaurant recommendation model (XGBoost + behavioral features), +6% CTR
· Migrated training pipelines to Kubeflow, cutting iteration time from days to hours

Research Assistant — Max Planck Institute for Informatics · Saarbrücken
Sep 2016 - Dec 2019
· PhD thesis on cross-lingual embedding alignment
· 4 publications at EMNLP, ACL, NAACL

EDUCATION

PhD in Computer Science — Saarland University · 2016-2020
MSc Computational Linguistics — University of Edinburgh · 2014-2016
BSc Computer Science — Fudan University · 2010-2014

SKILLS
Python, PyTorch, HuggingFace Transformers, scikit-learn, XGBoost
Elasticsearch, FAISS, Weaviate, PostgreSQL
AWS (SageMaker, EC2, S3), Docker, Kubernetes, Kubeflow
SQL, Spark, Airflow

SELECTED PUBLICATIONS
Chen, S. et al. (2019) "Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Retrieval via Anchor Languages." EMNLP.
Chen, S. & Müller, K. (2018) "Contrastive Alignment of Multilingual Word Embeddings." ACL.

LANGUAGES
English (native), Mandarin (native), German (C1), French (B1)

Manchester, UK | 07423 558 901 | olivia.wright.data@gmail.com

ABOUT ME

Former secondary school maths teacher (7 years) now working as a data analyst. I bring strong analytical rigour and the ability to explain complex findings to non-technical audiences — a skill built through a decade of teaching teenagers.

Personal projects on GitHub: analysis of UK school performance data, football expected goals model.
